reviews.review_msg: Nice prof but she grades papers on an unreachable scale. Her lectures are very informative and engaging, but it's content heavy. If you have no basic understanding of ancient history, this is NOT the course for you!
Very dry and long lectures.  Attendance is a must and weekly readings are a must.  If you take good notes, have good memorization skills and come to class, this course is an easy A.

dr mirhady was one awesome guy.. but his lectures were so god damn dry, especially for the material we were covering. the course was very fairly marked.. ended up with a B+ but barely studied. YOU BE THE JUDGE

He is really interested in what he teaches and tries to engage everyone in class. For a 3 hour lecture it seemed to drag on sometimes, but his exams are fair and relatively easy if you read the books and reviewed your notes. He is helpful in office hours as well. The only problem was that the grading scale seemed a bit off from the normal scale.

Definitely gives interesting lectures, is quite demanding as a tutorial leader (don't just DO the readings, write notes on them and analyze the information before you come). Exams don't seem difficult but his standards are extremely high. As a straight A student, I struggled to get an A-. But you will NEVER be bored. Great prof.

Very nice and helpful prof, but WHO SAID HE WAS EASY? The midterm and final consisted of memorizing dates after dates and unless you know your material solid, the exams are very tricky. His essay questions on the final killed! Easy to do decently but hard to excel in this class. Also very heavy reading every week.
